text.title: Reasoning Models: Comparison, Pricing, and Selection
text.summary: Comparison of reasoning models (o3, o4-mini, Claude Extended Thinking, DeepSeek R1), pricing, benchmark performance, and selection rules.

rule.statement: Use o4-mini for cost-effective reasoning tasks (math, code review, logic). Use Claude Extended Thinking when long context (200K+) or Anthropic API integration is required. Use DeepSeek R1 for budget-critical offline batch tasks.
rule.rationale: o3 and Claude 4 Opus are 10-50x more expensive than standard models. o4-mini at $1.10/M input with reasoning provides 83% of o3 quality at 55% of the cost. DeepSeek R1 at $0.55/M input is the cheapest option with visible reasoning tokens.

list.item: o3: $2.00/M input, $8.00/M output + reasoning billed as output, 200K context, private CoT
o4-mini: $1.10/M input, $4.40/M output, 200K context, private CoT
Claude 4 Opus: $15.00/M input, $75.00/M output, 200K context, Extended Thinking
Claude 4 Sonnet: $3.00/M input, $15.00/M output, Extended Thinking
DeepSeek R1: $0.55/M input, $2.19/M output, 128K context, visible think tags

section.title: Performance Benchmarks (2025)
section.p: Key benchmarks showing where reasoning models excel over standard models:

list.item: AIME 2024 (math): o3=83.3%, DeepSeek R1=79.8%, Claude 4 ET=75.0%, GPT-4o=13.4%
GPQA Diamond (science): o3=87.7%, Claude 4 ET=84.8%, DeepSeek R1=71.5%, GPT-4o=53.6%
SWE-bench (code): o3=71.7%, Claude 4 ET=72.5%, DeepSeek R1=49.2%, GPT-4o=33.2%

rule.statement: Always verify that a reasoning model outperforms a standard model on your specific task before committing to reasoning model costs. Run eval on 50-100 representative examples first.
rule.rationale: Reasoning models excel at formal logic, math, and code with edge cases. For tasks like creative writing, summarization, or simple retrieval, standard models often match quality at 10-50x lower cost per call.

rule.statement: Set thinking budget by task type: 1K-4K tokens for formatting/extraction, 4K-8K for code review and logic, 8K-32K for architecture planning, 32K-128K for deep research and theorem proving.
rule.rationale: Thinking budget is an upper bound — the model decides how much to use. Setting it too low truncates reasoning on complex tasks; setting it too high wastes cost on simple tasks. Empirically calibrate budgets per task type using eval data.
